question: How can swarm sense better? What sensing gaps exist in orient.py?
human_signal: swarm how can swarm sense better swarm

gaps_found.name: Signal sort inversion
gaps_found.description: orient.py sorted open signals newest-first within priority. Oldest P1 signals (SIG-1, SIG-2, age 55s) were buried below recent P1 signals in top-5 display.
gaps_found.impact: 55-session-old P1 directives invisible to every session
gaps_found.fix: Reversed sort to oldest-first within priority (descending age)

gaps_found.name: Backlog blindness
gaps_found.description: No alert fired when signals accumulated without resolution. 16/26 signals >20 sessions old with no warning.
gaps_found.impact: Swarm sensed individual signals but not accumulation rate
gaps_found.fix: Added BACKLOG alert when >20-session-old signals exist, with P1 callout

gaps_found.name: Knowledge state not wired to primary sensor
gaps_found.description: knowledge_state.py computed BLIND-SPOT 15.8% and DECAYED 20.6% but orient.py never read this output.
gaps_found.impact: Fundamental self-knowledge metric (16% of knowledge unreachable) invisible at session start
gaps_found.fix: Added cache read from experiments/meta/knowledge-state-s*.json into Scientific Rigor section

root_cause: Measurement tools exist but aren't wired into primary sense organ. L-601 structural enforcement: unread outputs decay to log files.
prescription: P14: every new measurement tool must wire output into orient.py or a periodic that feeds orient.py. Wiring IS the sensing act.
expected: Identify 3+ specific sensing gaps; wire knowledge_state into orient.py; add signal-age alert; write lesson
